title: QA Area Quantization FAIL: inherits_from='QA_CORE_SPEC.v0' (wrong version) → INVALID_KERNEL_REFERENCE
_fixture_purpose: Demonstrates INVALID_KERNEL_REFERENCE: inherits_from is set to 'QA_CORE_SPEC.v0' (a non-existent prior version) instead of the required 'QA_CORE_SPEC.v1'. The validator detects this and emits INVALID_KERNEL_REFERENCE. This is the canonical inheritance failure mode: a family extension that points to the wrong kernel cannot be validated against the actual kernel contract. The gate_policy_inherited and failure_algebra checks are still structurally correct in this cert — only the kernel reference itself is wrong. This proves the validator checks the reference before accepting any other compatibility claims.

quadrea_claim.forbidden_reason: 3 is inert in Z[phi]; f ≡ 0 mod 3 iff 3|b and 3|e.

generators.name: sigma
generators.action: e → e + 1 (mod 9); updates d = b+e mod 9, a = e+d mod 9

validation_checks.description: quadrea_claim.spectrum matches computed f(b,e) mod 9 for all b,e
validation_checks.details: spectrum claim correct despite bad kernel ref

fail_ledger.message: inherits_from='QA_CORE_SPEC.v0' is not a recognised kernel version. The only valid kernel reference for family_extension certs is 'QA_CORE_SPEC.v1'. A cert pointing to a non-existent or superseded kernel cannot be validated against the kernel contract: its compatibility claims (gate_policy_inherited, failure_algebra_extends_not_replaces, etc.) cannot be machine-verified. Correct by setting inherits_from='QA_CORE_SPEC.v1'.
